title: Creating Game Shortcuts (WiiGSC)
requirements: A Windows computer (WiiGSC only runs on Windows)
YAWM ModMii Edition (installs the resulting WAD on-console)
An SD card or USB drive, FAT32 formatted
A dumped Wii/GameCube backup and a USB loader already set up
warnings: !!Install Priiloader and BootMii before doing this!! A bad channel install can cause a banner brick that stops the Wii Menu from loading.
!!Do NOT!! create a shortcut channel for Mario Party 9 or A Boy and His Blob — both are known to crash the system.
_note: WiiGSC creates a forwarder channel that launches a specific game backup straight from the Wii Menu, without going through your USB loader's game list.

steps.title: Before you start
steps.body: **WiiGSC** is a desktop tool — the channel gets built on your PC, and only the finished `.wad` file goes onto your Wii. First, let's check your SD card or USB drive is FAT32.

steps.title: Download WiiGSC
steps.body:
On your Windows PC, download WiiGSC from `https://github.com/modmii/WiiGSC/releases/download/1.1/WiiGSC.zip` and extract it.

Launch `WiiGSC.exe`. On first run it asks permission to create a common key, with a disclaimer — this is expected, go ahead and proceed through the prompts.

steps.title: Create the channel
steps.body:
1. Choose **Open WBFS Drive** to browse a device with your backups, or select an individual ISO/WBFS file directly.
2. Pick the game you want a shortcut for and click **Use for Channel Creation**.
3. Select the USB loader you actually use (USB Loader GX, WiiFlow, Nintendont, etc.).
4. Click **Create Channel**. WiiGSC builds a `.wad` file for that game.

steps.title: Copy the WAD to your SD card
steps.body: Create a folder named `wad` on the root of your SD card or USB drive (if it doesn't already exist), and copy the `.wad` file WiiGSC just created into it.

steps.title: Clean up and eject the card
steps.body:
macOS scatters hidden junk files (`.DS_Store`, `._` files) onto SD cards, and Wii homebrew can choke on them.

Press the button, and Graphite strips those files off the card and **safely ejects it for you**. Once it pops off, pull the card out.

steps.title: Move the SD card to your Wii
steps.body: Put the SD card or USB drive into your Wii and power it on. Press **I did this** once it's in.

steps.title: Install the channel
steps.body:
Open your WAD manager (for example **YAWM ModMii Edition**, launched from the Homebrew Channel), locate the `.wad` file you just copied over, and install it.

Once it's done, the game's shortcut appears on your Wii Menu — select it to launch straight into that game.

troubleshoot.label: The channel doesn't work or the Wii Menu looks wrong

steps.title: You're done
steps.body: Your new shortcut channel launches that game directly from the Wii Menu. Repeat this guide for any other games you want a shortcut for.
